overview.short: Devaragundi, meaning 'God's pool', is a waterfall in India.
overview.description: Devaragundi, meaning 'God's pool', is a waterfall in India. It is created by one of the mountain streams at Thodikana. It can be reached by going further for about 1.5 kilometres from Sri Mallikarjuna Temple towards the Patti Hills. One must cross two streams, climb uphill, and walk through a private arecanut farm to view this magnificent waterfall.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Thodikana
topPlaces.description: Thodikana is an Indian village nestled in the foothills of the Western Ghats in southern Karnataka. It is in the Sullia taluk of Dakshina Kannada district, about 100 km from Mangalore city.

topPlaces.name: Sampaje
topPlaces.description: Sampaje is a district border village of Kodagu in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Madikeri taluk.

topPlaces.name: Aramboor
topPlaces.description: Aramboor is a small village in the Sullia taluk of the Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka, India. It is famous for its hanging bridge, Mookambika bajana mandira, Badar Juma Masjid, Govt Primary School, Payashwini River, Vayanat kulavan daivastana.

topPlaces.name: Brahmagiri Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: The Brahmagiri Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Kodagu District, Karnataka State, India, within the Western Ghats and about 250 km from Bengaluru. The sanctuary derives its name from the highest peak of the mountain range, Brahmagiri Peak.

items.note: Thodikana is an Indian village nestled in the foothills of the Western Ghats in southern Karnataka. It is in the Sullia taluk of Dakshina Kannada district, about 100 km from Mangalore city.
Sampaje is a district border village of Kodagu in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Madikeri taluk.

Aramboor is a small village in the Sullia taluk of the Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka, India. It is famous for its hanging bridge, Mookambika bajana mandira, Badar Juma Masjid, Govt Primary School, Payashwini River, Vayanat kulavan daivastana.

The Brahmagiri Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Kodagu District, Karnataka State, India, within the Western Ghats and about 250 km from Bengaluru. The sanctuary derives its name from the highest peak of the mountain range, Brahmagiri Peak.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Devaragundi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Devaragundi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (84 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (82 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Devaragundi?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Devaragundi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Devaragundi?
faq.a: Stays in Devaragundi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Devaragundi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thodikana, Sampaje, Aramboor, Brahmagiri Wildlife Sanctuary.
